relationship_to_restored_text: Interpretive contemplative layer aligned by verse. This does not replace the source-audited restored translation.

verses.mystical_translation: Job answers Bildad’s small speech with sharp irony.
What help has this been to the powerless?
What wisdom has this added to the one in need?
Job asks whose breath is really speaking through these words.
Even the shades tremble beneath the waters.
Sheol and Abaddon are uncovered before the Divine.
The earth is hung over nothing.
Waters are bound in clouds without tearing them.
Cloud can cover the moon’s face.
A boundary is drawn between light and darkness over the waters.
The pillars of heaven tremble at rebuke.
Sea and Rahab are stilled and shattered by power and understanding.
Breath clears the heavens, and the hand pierces the fleeing serpent.
All this is only the edge and whisper; who can understand the thunder?
